key_insight: A transfer-learning + hybrid Cross-Entropy/Lovász optimization makes BiSeNetV2 far more robust for reflective weld seam segmentation without increasing model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight, real-time perception upgrade for construction welding robots operating under harsh lighting and specular metal reflections.
method: BiSeNetV2 backbone enhanced via transfer learning
Hybrid Cross-Entropy + Lovász loss to stabilize seam learning
Focused on reflection-robust optimization rather than adding architectural depth
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown on seam segmentation benchmarks, but deployment may still require careful tuning for unseen surface finishes, lighting setups, and camera geometries.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
